topic: What to look for when buying a used golf cart
primary_keyword: what to look for when buying a used golf cart

brief.summary: Create a practical buyer-focused guide that explains what to look for when buying a used golf cart, with a strong emphasis on inspection, total ownership costs, and how to compare listings before making an offer. The article should help readers spot hidden wear, verify battery and drivetrain condition, assess frame/body/rust damage, and decide whether a cart is a good value for personal, utility, or fleet use. Position Jeff Martin Auctioneers through clear, trustworthy conversion language and CTAs that encourage readers to browse inventory, compare available carts, or contact the team for help finding the right used cart through JMA Golf Carts.
outline: H1: What to Look for When Buying a Used Golf Cart
H2: Start with the Right Use Case: Personal, Utility, Fleet, or Street-Legal
H2: Used Golf Cart Buying Checklist: The Core Inspection Points
H2: What to Inspect on Batteries, Charger, Motor, and Drivetrain
H2: Frame, Suspension, Tires, Brakes, and Body Damage to Watch For
H2: Questions to Ask the Seller Before You Buy Used Golf Cart Online
H2: How to Compare Condition, Age, and Price Across Listings
H2: Where to Find Trusted Used Golf Carts and When to Contact Jeff Martin Auctioneers

seo_strategy.search_intent: Commercial investigation: users want a buyer guide that helps them evaluate used golf carts, avoid costly mistakes, compare listings, and feel confident purchasing online or through a marketplace.
seo_strategy.cluster: used golf cart buying and inspection guidance
seo_strategy.opportunity_rationale: High buyer-intent early-funnel topic with broad demand, strong checklist utility, and natural pathways into inventory, comparisons, and contact CTAs.
